The model favours Portugal at 66%, with Uzbekistan's win probability at 9%. The sportsbook consensus is considerably more confident in a Portugal victory, placing it at 79%, leaving the underdog just 7% implied chance across the two consensus sources.

The model's W/D/L distribution suggests a moderately competitive match. The draw probability of 25% indicates meaningful uncertainty about the outcome; it is neither a foregone conclusion nor a narrow affair. Portugal's edge is substantial but not overwhelming under the statistical framework.

Model and market diverge noticeably here. The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket both assign Portugal roughly 13 percentage points higher win probability than the model does, and compress the draw likelihood to around 14%. This represents a meaningful gap. The dashboard does not adjudicate which reading is more accurate—the discrepancy may reflect information or risk preferences not captured in the statistical model.
